title: lecanopteris scandens
intro: Lecanopteris scandens, also known as the climbing fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits associated with Lecanopteris scandens include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the leaves in hot water or ethanol, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
